guide_review_response.summary: Iteration 1 of Stage 2 EA instance 3 comprehension-gate review. Pickup verification (V0a-V0g) is clean. All 16 binding acknowledgments are restated verbatim. Comprehension-gate structure correctly identifies items 2.4-2.10 scope (no redo of 2.1-2.3). Defaults-preservation, single- commit, fleet-pause, and chokepoint disciplines all captured. Eight corrections required before execution. Three HIGH (PS1 enumeration gaps + parameter-name standardization), four MED (vague Python enumeration + C4/C6 + risk-label hygiene + rollback acknowledgment), one LOW (w3 preflight watchpoint). The EA understood the stage; these are correctable in a re-emit. NOT REJECTED — ack discipline is solid and pickup verification is clean. NOT APPROVED — HIGH defects would result in workflow-integrity work being skipped (G-1/G-4 PS1 set incomplete) and a parameter-naming drift that contradicts the canonical 2.4.v2 signature pattern.

correction.issue: Item 2.5 PowerShell (PS1) enumeration is missing two scripts from the Stage 2 XML 2.4.v2 additional_tools list: `tools/fleet_observability/escalation_notify.ps1` (note: third directory, not in the EA's audit scope) and `tools/scheduled-tasks/test_async_post_gate.ps1`. The EA's audit scope ("every .ps1 in tools/scheduled-tasks/ and tools/fleet_ops/") would not catch escalation_notify.ps1 because it lives under tools/fleet_observability/.
correction.source_citation: 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml <v2_updates> <item id="2.4.v2"> <additional_tools> — lists 9 tools total; EA captured 7. Missing entries explicitly listed with parameter requirements.
correction.required_action: Add to item 2.5 work plan: `tools/fleet_observability/escalation_notify.ps1` and `tools/scheduled-tasks/test_async_post_gate.ps1`. Expand the PS1 audit scope from two directories to three — include `tools/fleet_observability/*.ps1`.

correction.issue: la_merge_approve.ps1 path is wrong. EA wrote "tools/fleet_ops/la_merge_approve.ps1 (line 38)". Actual location is `tools/scheduled-tasks/la_merge_approve.ps1`. The EA copied the v1 item 2.5 `<files_identified_in_phase_2>` path which is itself a stale reference; the v2 update 2.4.v2 has the correct path. WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW Appendix A.1 sweep confirms the actual location.
correction.required_action: Correct the path to `tools/scheduled-tasks/la_merge_approve.ps1` in item 2.5 work plan and risk list. Do NOT look for the file at tools/fleet_ops/ — it has never lived there.

correction.source_citation: 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml <v2_updates> <item id="2.4.v2"> <signature_pattern_for_ps1> (canonical) and <item id="2.5.v2"> <signature> (wake_launcher); WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md §3 G-1 fix #1 ("rename `$RepoRoot` parameter in the 4 scripts to `$BlarAIRoot` ... mirroring wake_launcher.ps1 §2.5.v2 signature").
correction.required_action: Standardize on `-BlarAIRoot` (with internal `$BlarAIRoot` variable) for ALL 7 PS1 parameters across items 2.5 + 2.5.v2: wake_launcher.ps1, agents-cadence-monitor.ps1, escalation_watchdog.ps1, toast_watchdog.ps1, la_merge_approve.ps1, escalation_notify.ps1, test_async_post_gate.ps1. Default value: `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })`. For the 4 G-1 scripts: also rename internal `$RepoRoot` references to `$BlarAIRoot` per 2.4.v2 per-tool descriptions.

correction.issue: Item 2.4 Python entrypoint list ends with "...other entrypoints" — not specific enough for verification. Successor must enumerate the precise file set so each can be independently checked for argparse flags. The legacy `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` (a load_config caller per v2-1) is also the subject of the legacy-name-collision-watch decision in item 2.6 — surface the overlap explicitly so the same file isn't refactored inconsistently in 2.4 vs 2.6.
correction.source_citation: EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v2.xml <midflight_observations> <obs from="v2" id="v2-1"> — explicitly lists 9 caller files / 11 invocations of load_config: tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py, tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py, daily_digest.py (×2), dashboard_maintainer.py, weekly_summary.py (×2), welcome_back_digest.py, tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (legacy), tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py, tools/gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py. Plus 2.4.v2 additional Python tools: tools/autonomy_budget/blarai_next_task_resolver.py, tools/fleet_ops/diff_builder.py, tools/autonomy_budget/active_tasks.py.
correction.required_action: Replace "...other entrypoints" with the explicit 12-file list (9 from v2-1 + 3 from 2.4.v2). Note in the work plan that `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` (legacy) is touched by BOTH item 2.4 (load_config caller) AND item 2.6 (legacy-name-collision-watch decision); ensure consistent treatment across both.

correction.issue: C4 and C6 commitments are vague ("to be confirmed in v2-aware reading; will surface during 2.4/2.6"). C4 requires explicit inclusion of `tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py` MCP handlers in item 2.4 — the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server bypasses _vikunja_client.py if not refactored, so an LLM can still issue scope-leaking queries from a Claude session. C6 requires extending the grep patterns at item 2.6 beyond the 3 in the XML.
correction.source_citation: AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md §1 C4 ("Stage 2.4 enumerates CLI tools to refactor but omits tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py ... If list_tasks and search_tasks MCP tool handlers don't route through _vikunja_client, an LLM can still issue scope-leaking queries from a Claude session") and §1 C6 ("Misses: httpx, aiohttp, urllib, 127.0.0.1:3456, env-var-driven base URLs (os.environ['VIKUNJA_URL']), templated f-strings (f'{base}/api/v1/tasks/all'), websocket clients").
correction.required_action: (a) Add `tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py` and any FastMCP/JSON-RPC handlers to item 2.4 audit list — every Vikunja-touching MCP tool handler must route through `tools/_vikunja_client.py`. (b) Extend item 2.6 grep patterns beyond the XML's 3 patterns: add `httpx`, `aiohttp`, `urllib`, `127.0.0.1:3456`, env-var URLs (`os.environ["VIKUNJA_URL"]`, `$env:VIKUNJA_URL`), templated f-strings (`f"{base}/api/v1/..."`), and websocket clients. Triage every hit; do not pre-filter.

correction.issue: Risk-label hygiene. The EA labels two risks as "V1 (outstanding)" and "V8 (outstanding)" where V1 and V8 in AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md §0.5 are reserved for "Worktree contamination" and "Doctrine split race" respectively. The actual EA concerns are "argparse-conflict-watch" and "load_config-defaults-preservation regression" per the predecessor's risk_label_hygiene_carryforward block. This is the same defect class the predecessor (EA instance 2) was warned about during iteration-1 review with Guide instance 3 — they used "R5"/"R6" colliding with AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW §0.6 R5/R6. Same principle applies to V-prefix.
correction.source_citation: EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v2.xml <risk_label_hygiene_carryforward> ("Successor MUST use distinct, descriptive labels for any new risk concerns rather than recycling numeric R-prefixed identifiers"); AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md §0.5 V1 (Worktree contamination) and V8 (Doctrine split race).
correction.required_action: Relabel "V1 (outstanding)" → "argparse-conflict-watch"; "V8 (outstanding)" → "load_config-defaults-preservation-watch". Apply this discipline to any future risk concern: descriptive labels only, no numeric overlap with AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW V1-V8 / R1-R12 / C1-C10 / F1-F5 / G-1 to G-4 / S-1 to S-3, and no overlap with verification-block IDs V0a-V0g / V1-V7 / V8.

correction.issue: Stage 2 XML <rollback> block is not explicitly acknowledged. The comprehension-gate review axis "rollback_awareness" states "For Stages 2 and 4, this is mandatory". EA's "single-commit discipline" mention covers what happens if context exhausts before 2.9, but not what happens if post-2.9 verification fails.
correction.source_citation: 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml <rollback> ("git reset --hard HEAD~1 (on chore/platform-extraction branch)" or "git revert HEAD if the commit has already been pushed -- don't push this branch yet -- keep local"); GUIDE_PROMPT.xml <ea_comprehension_gate_review> <axis name="rollback_awareness"> ("For Stages 2 and 4, this is mandatory").
correction.required_action: Add an explicit acknowledgment: "If post-item-2.9 verification fails (V1-V7 closure or per-item V8), rollback is `git reset --hard HEAD~1` on chore/platform-extraction (level-1 per RECOVERY.md; branch is local, not pushed). Branch is preserved for audit per copilot-instructions preservation_rule. Fleet stays disabled — no runtime impact from rollback."

correction.issue: The `$BLARAI_PID` preflight at item 2.8 is the FIRST REAL INVOCATION of the cleanup-C3-corrected regex `'blarai_project_id:\s*(\d+)'` in the entire procedure. EA's plan mentions resolving `$BLARAI_PID = 3` from vikunja_project_ids.yaml but does not flag the watchpoint that an empty (or non-3) result requires STOP and report — not silent regex/yaml adaptation.
correction.source_citation: GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml <watchpoint id="w3"> ("First REAL invocation will be Stage 2.8 -- if it returns empty, the regex or yaml schema still drifted. Surface as required_correction immediately and bounce to LA"); STATUS.md Anomaly A6 (cleanup C1+C3 generalization).
correction.required_action: Add to risk list: "$BLARAI_PID preflight at item 2.8 is the first real invocation of the cleanup-C3-corrected regex `'blarai_project_id:\s*(\d+)'` against `BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml`. Expected value: 3. If the regex returns empty OR a value other than 3, STOP and report to Lead Architect — do NOT silently adapt the regex or modify the yaml. The yaml is the source of truth for the schema convention; deviation indicates an upstream drift that must be diagnosed."

required_acknowledgments.acknowledge: I will incorporate all 8 corrections into a re-emitted comprehension gate before any item 2.4 work begins.
I will use `-BlarAIRoot` (not `-ProjectRoot`) for all 7 PS1 parameters per v2 canonical signature pattern at 2.4.v2.

I will explicitly enumerate the 12 Python entrypoints (9 load_config callers from v2-1 + 3 v2.4.v2 additions) and the 7 PS1 scripts (with la_merge_approve at the correct `tools/scheduled-tasks/` path).
I will include `tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py` MCP handlers in item 2.4 audit and extend item 2.6 grep patterns per C4/C6 source citations.
If item 2.8 `$BLARAI_PID` preflight returns empty or any value other than 3, I will STOP and report to Lead Architect — no silent regex or yaml adaptation.
I will use descriptive risk labels (no numeric prefix overlap with AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW or verification-block IDs).
I will not begin item 2.4 work, create branches, or run mutation commands until the re-emitted gate is reviewed and explicitly authorized by Guide instance 4.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: HOLD — re-emit your comprehension gate addressing required_corrections, then await Guide re-review.
